# opencode-smartsnip

> opencode plugin that routes shell commands through the snip CLI token filter.
> Allowlist-driven: only commands matching snip's filter table are wrapped; everything
> else passes through byte-identical. Validated on 23,917 real opencode commands.

## What it does

- Hooks `tool.execute.before` for the bash tool and rewrites wrap-eligible commands
  from `git status` to `snip git status` before execution.
- snip (https://github.com/edouard-claude/snip) filters the output 60-90% before it
  enters the session.
- Anything uncertain (heredocs, control flow, `$( )`, pipes-downstream, unknown
  commands, builtins) passes through untouched.

## For agents running under this plugin

- Output of filtered commands is compressed but information-preserving.
- If a command output contains `[full output: /path/to/file.log]`, the raw
  uncompressed output is in that file — Read it instead of re-running the command.
- Append `#nosnip` to any bash command to get its full raw output.
- `git log --format=...`, `git status --porcelain` etc. are never filtered — explicit
  format flags are respected.

## Files

- README.md — install, configuration, design
- RESEARCH.md — evidence base: ecosystem survey, 23k-command corpus analysis
- src/parser.ts — shell-aware top-level splitter
- src/router.ts — wrap decision logic
- src/filters.ts + src/builtin-filters.ts — allowlist from snip's filter table
- bin/smartsnip.ts — `smartsnip discover` (missed-savings report from opencode
  history) and `smartsnip doctor` (setup verification)

## Config

`~/.config/opencode/smartsnip.json` or `.opencode/smartsnip.json`:
`{ enabled, deny[], allow[], snipPath, scanUserFilters, toast }`
Default deny (data channels): ssh, curl, wget, psql, jq.
